hero.headline: Builds things that ship.
hero.subline: I design, build, and run my own products: web, native iOS and macOS, backend, and the infrastructure under them.

about.lead: I like building complete things: I design them, build the web and native apps, write the backend, and run the servers they ship on. Everything on this site is live and in use.
body: The day job is engineering at Kharetah, a UAE fintech, where I work across product engineering, LLM-powered internal tooling, the Azure cloud, security, and IT.

The personal work here is all in production: a couples app with a zero-dependency Node backend and a native SwiftUI client submitted to the App Store, a self-hosted streaming platform with a custom sync protocol, and a signed, notarized macOS utility.

I trained in Computer Science and started in design. In practice that means I'll hand-roll drag-and-drop from raw pointer events or tune an HLS pipeline for iOS quirks, and I care whether the type is right.

themes.title: End to end
themes.detail: Idea, design, code, infrastructure, release. Each project here went through all of it.

themes.title: In production
themes.detail: Real use, uptime, migrations, and bug fixes from daily driving, not demos.

themes.title: Below the framework
themes.detail: Zero-dependency servers, custom sync protocols, raw pointer events, scrypt auth without a library.

smallerBuilds.name: Flixify
smallerBuilds.line: Native macOS drag-and-drop transcoder that preps uploads for PookieFlix: hardware VideoToolbox H.264, bundled ffmpeg, shipped as a signed and notarized DMG.

smallerBuilds.name: PerfectTasks
smallerBuilds.line: Full-stack task manager (Next.js, Drizzle, Clerk) designed, planned, and built test-first in a single day.

smallerBuilds.name: orbit-core
smallerBuilds.line: NestJS + Prisma backend for time management: OAuth, Google Calendar sync, multi-device conflict resolution, BullMQ jobs.
